raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":0.7,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":0.7,"RX.5":0.7},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"Improving the draft centrally includes refining the contribution claim and framing of TRIAGE-MT beyond sentence-level edits.","R1.2":"The request implies checking whether the fixed-point/convergence reasoning and conclusions follow coherently from the stated premises.","R1.3":"The draft makes empirical claims from reported COMET, chrF, and convergence results, so improvement requires aligning conclusions with those data.","R1.5":"The draft's narrative arc from system description to results, convergence interpretation, and recommendation may need section-level ordering improvements.","R1.6":"Writing polish is load-bearing because a generic request to improve a draft most directly entails clearer, tighter, better-flowing prose.","RX.1":"Any projected improvement work should preserve uncertainty around empirical interpretation and generality.","RX.2":"The strong claims about genuine error recovery, general-purpose utility, and drop-in use make overclaim avoidance essential.","RX.3":"The delegation is only to improve the draft, so projected responsibility must stay within draft-improvement tasks rather than executing experiments or rewriting the artifact here.","RX.4":"Recommendations about translation-system deployment could affect downstream use, so harms from overstated reliability or fidelity should be considered.","RX.5":"The projection should preserve traceability to the reported metrics, setup, and claims in the artifact."}}

rationale.R1.1: Improving the draft centrally includes refining the contribution claim and framing of TRIAGE-MT beyond sentence-level edits.
rationale.R1.2: The request implies checking whether the fixed-point/convergence reasoning and conclusions follow coherently from the stated premises.
rationale.R1.3: The draft makes empirical claims from reported COMET, chrF, and convergence results, so improvement requires aligning conclusions with those data.
rationale.R1.5: The draft's narrative arc from system description to results, convergence interpretation, and recommendation may need section-level ordering improvements.
rationale.R1.6: Writing polish is load-bearing because a generic request to improve a draft most directly entails clearer, tighter, better-flowing prose.
rationale.RX.1: Any projected improvement work should preserve uncertainty around empirical interpretation and generality.
rationale.RX.2: The strong claims about genuine error recovery, general-purpose utility, and drop-in use make overclaim avoidance essential.
rationale.RX.3: The delegation is only to improve the draft, so projected responsibility must stay within draft-improvement tasks rather than executing experiments or rewriting the artifact here.
rationale.RX.4: Recommendations about translation-system deployment could affect downstream use, so harms from overstated reliability or fidelity should be considered.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should preserve traceability to the reported metrics, setup, and claims in the artifact.
